Chapter 2 — CLAUDE.md: Your Coding Const — CLAUDE.md is the file Claude reads at the start of every session. It is the difference between a Claude that knows your

data.title: OPENING
blocks: The teacher started their second Claude Code session for the class-website project. They had the /init-generated CLAUDE.
Opening — They asked Claude to add a Contact page to the site.

data.title: WHAT CLAUDE.MD IS.
blocks: Project-root CLAUDE.md and .claude/CLAUDE.md project-specific instructions.
User-level ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md your personal preferences across all projects.
Managed policy locations (for districts and organizations that enforce policies) overrides that apply across all users on the device.

S04.meta: // SLIDE 04 — WHAT GOES IN: THE FIVE ELEMENT

data.title: WHAT GOES IN: THE FIVE ELEMENTS
blocks: Bash commands Claude cannot guess.
Code style deviations from the norm.
Test runners and verification commands.
Architectural decisions.
Environment quirks.
data.section: WHAT GOES IN: THE FIVE ELEMENT

data.title: WHAT STAYS OUT.
blocks: CLAUDE.md include — exclude
code style deviations — A concrete checkpoint for applying the chapter concept.
test runners — A concrete checkpoint for applying the chapter concept.

S07.meta: // SLIDE 07 — CLAUDE.MD VS. SKILLS VS. HOOKS

data.title: CLAUDE.MD VS. SKILLS VS. HOOKS
blocks: · Must Claude know this every session, but can violate it occasionally without catastrophe? → CLAUDE.md.
· Is this a workflow Claude runs sometimes, and I want the workflow's specifics out of the always-on context? → Skill.
· Must this rule hold every time without exception? → Hook.

data.title: /MEMORY: THE TOUR
blocks: Claude Code includes a /memory command that lists all CLAUDE.md files currently loaded in your session. Useful when you
/memory: the tour — /memory also lets you edit the files in place. You can update an entry, save, and the change is reflected in the next pr

S09.meta: // SLIDE 09 — TEACHER BUILD: WRITE YOUR CLAS

data.title: TEACHER BUILD: WRITE YOUR CLASS-WEBSITE CLAUDE.MD
blocks: · Open /init's output. Read top to bottom.
· Refine each entry. Cut what Claude can figure out from code. Tighten the generalities. Move volatile content out.
· Add the missing categories. Architectural decisions you have made; environment quirks for your school; the never-rules f
· Add a Lessons Learned section. Initially empty or with one entry. It will grow.
· Verify under 200 lines. If longer, audit for what should move to a Skill (Chapter 6).

data.title: CLASSROOM ACTIVITY: STUDENTS WRITE THEIR OWN CLAUDE.MD
blocks: Refine it cut three entries Claude could figure out from the code; tighten two vague entries with specifics.
Add three entries Claude cannot infer an architectural decision, an environment quirk, a never-rule.
Run a test session ask Claude to add a feature. Observe whether the output respects the refined CLAUDE.md.
Submit the refined CLAUDE.md, plus a one-paragraph reflection on which entries Claude respected and which it ignored.
